Package neqsim.process.operations

Class ControllerTuningStudy

java.lang.Object
neqsim.process.operations.ControllerTuningStudy
public final class ControllerTuningStudy extends Object
Utility methods for screening controller tuning from simulated or historian time series.

The study is deliberately independent of any specific controller implementation. Existing NeqSim controllers can generate the histories, and this class computes portable performance metrics for P&ID-driven operating studies.

Version:
1.0
Author:
ESOL

  • Constructor Details

    • ControllerTuningStudy

      private ControllerTuningStudy()
      Private constructor for utility class.

  • Method Details

    • evaluateStepResponse

      public static ControllerTuningResult evaluateStepResponse(String controllerName, double setPoint, double[] timeSeconds, double[] processValue, double[] controllerOutput, double outputMin, double outputMax, double settlingTolerance)
      Evaluates a controller response using standard time-domain performance metrics.
      Parameters:
      controllerName - controller name
      setPoint - target value for the controlled variable
      timeSeconds - monotonically increasing time samples in seconds
      processValue - controlled variable samples
      controllerOutput - controller output samples, typically valve opening or speed demand
      outputMin - lower output limit
      outputMax - upper output limit
      settlingTolerance - absolute tolerance for settling and final stability
      Returns:
      controller tuning result
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if arrays are null, too short, mismatched, or non-monotonic

    • validateInputs

      private static void validateInputs(double[] timeSeconds, double[] processValue, double[] controllerOutput, double outputMin, double outputMax, double settlingTolerance)
      Validates study inputs.
      Parameters:
      timeSeconds - time samples
      processValue - process-value samples
      controllerOutput - controller-output samples
      outputMin - lower output limit
      outputMax - upper output limit
      settlingTolerance - absolute settling tolerance
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if input data is invalid

    • meanAbsoluteError

      private static double meanAbsoluteError(double setPoint, double[] processValue)
      Calculates mean absolute error.
      Parameters:
      setPoint - target value
      processValue - process-value samples
      Returns:
      mean absolute error

    • maxAbsoluteError

      private static double maxAbsoluteError(double setPoint, double[] processValue)
      Calculates maximum absolute error.
      Parameters:
      setPoint - target value
      processValue - process-value samples
      Returns:
      maximum absolute error

    • integrateError

      private static double integrateError(double setPoint, double[] timeSeconds, double[] processValue, boolean squared)
      Integrates absolute or squared error using the trapezoidal rule.
      Parameters:
      setPoint - target value
      timeSeconds - time samples
      processValue - process-value samples
      squared - true to integrate squared error, false for absolute error
      Returns:
      integrated error

    • overshootPercent

      private static double overshootPercent(double setPoint, double[] processValue)
      Calculates overshoot relative to the requested step magnitude.
      Parameters:
      setPoint - target value
      processValue - process-value samples
      Returns:
      overshoot percent

    • settlingTime

      private static double settlingTime(double setPoint, double[] timeSeconds, double[] processValue, double tolerance)
      Finds the first time after which all samples stay inside tolerance.
      Parameters:
      setPoint - target value
      timeSeconds - time samples
      processValue - process-value samples
      tolerance - absolute tolerance
      Returns:
      settling time, or NaN when not settled

    • saturationFraction

      private static double saturationFraction(double[] controllerOutput, double outputMin, double outputMax)
      Calculates the fraction of output samples at actuator limits.
      Parameters:
      controllerOutput - controller-output samples
      outputMin - lower output limit
      outputMax - upper output limit
      Returns:
      fraction from 0 to 1

    • stableAtEnd

      private static boolean stableAtEnd(double setPoint, double[] processValue, double tolerance)
      Checks whether the final fifth of samples is stable.
      Parameters:
      setPoint - target value
      processValue - process-value samples
      tolerance - absolute tolerance
      Returns:
      true when final error and variation are within tolerance

    • recommendation

      private static String recommendation(boolean stableAtEnd, double overshootPercent, double settlingTimeSeconds, double outputSaturationFraction)
      Generates a short recommendation from metrics.
      Parameters:
      stableAtEnd - true if final response is stable
      overshootPercent - overshoot percent
      settlingTimeSeconds - settling time or NaN
      outputSaturationFraction - actuator saturation fraction
      Returns:
      recommendation text
